chunks.text: Towards a TMS- and MRI- Compatible Wireless EEG System by Yu-Chi Lin. Hydrogel Actuated Carbon Fiber Microelectrode Array (EECS-2023-59) by Oliver Chen. Design, Development and Applications of Portable Field Emission Devices (EECS-2021-224) by Nishita Deka, Vivek Subramanian and Kristofer Pister.
chunks.question: What are the titles of the first three technical reports?
chunks.answer: Wireless EEG, hydrogel microelectrode, portable emission devices.

chunks.text: Actuation and Localization of Resource-Constrained Autonomous Microrobotic Systems (EECS-2021-20) by Brian Kilberg. Robust and Unsupervised Interest Point Detection for Efficient Visual Odometry (EECS-2020-64) by Farhan Toddywala. Monolithic Wireless Transceiver Design (EECS-2020-33) by Filip Maksimovic.
chunks.question: What topics do the reports by Kilberg, Toddywala, and Maksimovic cover?
chunks.answer: Microrobotics, visual odometry, wireless transceiver design.

chunks.text: Modeling and Control of Resonant Switched Capacitor DC-DC Converter (EECS-2018-6) by Yongjun Li. Implantable Beta Radiation Detecting Active Pixel Sensor Design For Prostate Cancer Surveillance (EECS-2017-177) by Leticia Ibarra, Michel Maharbiz and Kristofer Pister. Implementation and Characterization of a Multi-hop 6TiSCH Network for Experimental Feedback Control of an Inverted Pendulum (EECS-2017-26) by Craig Schindler, Thomas Watteyne, Xavier Vilajosana and Kristofer Pister.
chunks.question: What are the applications of the reports by Li, Ibarra et al., and Schindler et al.?
chunks.answer: DC-DC converters, cancer detection, network control systems.

chunks.text: Applied Estimation of Mobile Environments (EECS-2014-32) by Kevin Weekly. Towards a Synthesizable Standard-Cell Radio (EECS-2012-219) by Richard Yu-Kuwan Su and Kristofer Pister. Outlet Power Monitoring Using Wireless Sensor Networks (EECS-2012-152) by Ameer Ellaboudy.
chunks.question: What do the reports by Weekly, Su and Pister, and Ellaboudy focus on?
chunks.answer: Mobile estimation, standard-cell radio, power monitoring.

chunks.text: Protocol-Independent Compression for Resource-Constrained Wireless Networks (EECS-2012-93) by Travis Massey and Kristofer Pister. RF Ranging for Location Awareness (EECS-2009-69) by Steven Michael Lanzisera and Kristofer Pister. Joystick Control for TinyOS Robot (M02/24) by M. Chiri, S. Bergbreiter and Kris Pister.
chunks.question: What technologies are addressed in the reports by Massey et al., Lanzisera et al., and Chiri et al.?
chunks.answer: Wireless compression, RF ranging, robot control.

chunks.text: Process Variation Analysis for MEMS Design (M00/44) by L. Schenato, W-C. Wu, Laurent El Ghaoui and Kristofer Pister. Wireless Communications for Smart Dust (M98/2) by V.S. Hsu, Joseph M. Kahn and Kristofer Pister. Finger-Like Biomechanical Robots (M92/12) by D.C. Deno, R.M. Murray, Kristofer Pister and S. Shankar Sastry. Control Primitives for Robot Systems (M90/39) by D.C. Deno, R.M. Murray, Kristofer Pister and S. Shankar Sastry.
chunks.question: What are the main themes of the oldest four technical reports?
chunks.answer: MEMS analysis, smart dust, biomechanical robots, robot control.
